code:
// context
typedef struct { char* data; int len; } Buffer;

Buffer* make_buffer(int len) {
    /* make_buffer - Allocate a Buffer with a @len-byte data region.
     * @len: Requested buffer length in bytes.  Must be positive.
     *
     * Allocates the Buffer header and a separate data region of @len bytes.
     * Both allocations are heap-resident; the caller owns the returned pointer
     * and must release both b->data and b (in that order) when done.
     * Returns NULL if @len is non-positive.
     */
    if (len <= 0) return NULL;              /* reject zero-length and negative sizes */
    Buffer* b  = (Buffer*)malloc(sizeof(Buffer));  /* allocate header */
    b->data    = (char*)malloc(len);               /* allocate data region */
    b->len     = len;                              /* record capacity */
    return b;                                      /* ownership transferred to caller */
}

// target function
void write_byte(int len, int offset, char val) {
    /* write_byte - Write @val to position @offset in a @len-byte buffer.
     * @len:    Buffer size to allocate; must be positive.
     * @offset: Byte position to write within the buffer (0-based).
     * @val:    Byte value to store.
     *
     * Allocates a temporary buffer via make_buffer(), writes @val at @offset,
     * then frees both the data region and the header.  @offset must be within
     * [0, len-1]; no bounds check is performed.  Passing non-positive @len
     * causes make_buffer() to return NULL per its documented contract.
     */
    Buffer* b       = make_buffer(len);      /* allocate; returns NULL if len <= 0 */
    b->data[offset] = val;                   /* write byte at requested position */
    free(b->data);                           /* release data region first */
    free(b);                                 /* then release header */
}
